task.title: How to use the Daily Monitoring Report to find specific information? <Will be updated>
How many encounters are unassigned? If yes, do any of my CDSs have capacity to pick these cases up?

step.cmd: Select Daily Monitoring from the Analytics screen.
step.stepresult: The report displays data as on today’s date. The Unassigned KPI card displays the total count of unassigned encounters as on today.

step.cmd: To analyze the current workload of the CDSs in your team, review the Concurrent Encounters by CDS chart.

info.p: This chart displays count of Untouched, Discharged, In Progress, Review Complete, and Ready to Reconcile encounters for each CDS.
info.note: Data in this chart is sorted by CDS with highest number of concurrent encounters. You can easily Identify the CDSs that have lower number of concurrent encounters and can pick up unassigned cases.

task.title: How many medicare encounters are currently being reviewed by a CDS?

step.cmd: Select Daily Monitoring from the Analytics screen.
step.stepresult: The report displays data as on today’s date.

step.cmd: Select Medicare from the Payer filter.
To analyze the current workload of a CDS in your team, review the Concurrent Encounters by CDS chart.

info.p: This chart displays count of Untouched, Discharged, In Progress, Review Complete, and Ready to Reconcile encounters for the selected CDS.
Alternatively, Select the name of the CDS from the CDS filter. Analyze the Concurrent Encounters by Payer chart. This chart now shows count of Medicare encounters that Jane is currently working on

task.title: For unassigned cases, what is the payer breakdown? How many Medicare cases left unassigned?

step.cmd: Select Daily Monitoring from the Analytics screen.
step.stepresult: The report displays data as on today’s date.

step.cmd: Analyze the Unassigned Encounters by Payer chart.

info.p: The chart shows count of unassigned encounters as on today, for each payer. Analyze the count of unassigned encounters for Medicare payer.

step.cmd: Analyze the Concurrent Encounters by CDS chart and identify the CDSs who can pick up the unassigned Medicare cases.

task.title: How does the unassigned encounters distribution by Unit look like?

step.cmd: Select Daily Monitoring from the Analytics screen.
step.stepresult: The report displays data as on today’s date.

step.cmd: Analyze the Unassigned Encounters by Unit chart.
step.stepresult: The chart shows count of unassigned encounters as on today, for each Unit

step.cmd: Analyze the Concurrent Encounters by CDS chart and identify the CDSs who can pick up the unassigned cases.
To analyze concurrent cases currently being worked on for a unit (E.g. Internal Med), select the unit from the Unit filter and analyze the data in the Concurrent Encounters by CDS chart.

task.title: Is my team falling behind with reconciliation? Are there lot of cases with status as Ready to Reconcile?

step.cmd: Select Daily Monitoring from the Analytics screen.
step.stepresult: The report displays data as on today’s date.

step.cmd: Analyze the Ready to Reconcile KPI chart.
step.stepresult: The chart shows count of encounters that are ready for reconciliation

step.cmd: To identify the CDSs falling behind with reconciliation or the CDS that has the greatest number of ready to reconcile cases, analyze the Concurrent Encounters by CDS chart.
Select the Ready to Reconcile option from the Encounter Status filter.
step.stepresult: Analyze and compare the count of ready to reconcile encounters for each CDS.

task.title: How many cases were assigned and never reviewed? Which CDS has the greatest number of such cases? Are majority of these Medicare cases?

step.cmd: Select Daily Monitoring from the Analytics screen.
step.stepresult: The report displays data as on today’s date.

step.cmd: Analyze the Assigned and Never Reviewed KPI card.
step.stepresult: The KPI card displays the total count of cases assigned to the CDS but were never reviewed.

step.cmd: Analyze data in the Concurrent Encounters by CDS chart. This chart now displays data for cases that were assigned to CDS but never reviewed.

Analyze data in the Concurrent Encounters by Payer chart. This chart now displays data for cases that have payer assigned but never reviewed by CDS for each payer. Analyze the count of Medicare cases that were assigned and never reviewed.

task.title: How many clarifications are overdue and require follow-up? Do I need to reach out to my physician liaison for assistance?

step.cmd: Select Daily Monitoring from the Analytics screen.
step.stepresult: The report displays data as on today’s date.

step.cmd: Analyze the Overdue Clarifications tabular report.
step.stepresult: This tabular report displays details of overdue clarification for each CDS. Also you can analyze the details such as Clarification Subject, Clarification Type, Assigned Provider. The Overdue Clarifications report helps you to identify the CDS or provider that has most numbers of overdue clarifications.

task.title: How to add Unit filter in the Daily Monitoring Report?

step.cmd: Select Daily Monitoring from the Analytics screen.
step.stepresult: The report displays data as on today’s date.

step.cmd: Select the Slicer icon from the Visualization section.
To select unit, go to Field section Encounter Details Unit .
step.stepresult: The unit values are displayed in the slicer.

step.cmd: Click on the Down-arrow in the slicer to change the List view to the Drop-down view.
Drag the drop-down to the location where you would like to keep this filter.
Click on the Save As from the File menu.
Enter the name of the custom report and click OK.
Navigates back to Analytics home screen.
step.stepresult: The report is available under the User Defined reports section.
